questions.q: What is the purpose of the COMMIT command in SQL?
questions.answer: The COMMIT command is used to save all the changes made by the current transaction.

questions.q: Which of the following SQL commands is used to add a new column to an existing table? (A) ALTER TABLE (B) CREATE TABLE (C) DROP TABLE (D) TRUNCATE TABLE
Explain the difference between the PRIMARY KEY and FOREIGN KEY constraints in SQL.
questions.answer: PRIMARY KEY uniquely identifies each record in a table, whereas FOREIGN KEY is a field in a table that is the PRIMARY KEY in another table.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The _______ clause is used to specify the conditions for which rows to include in the result set.
State True or False: The ROLLBACK command can be used to undo the changes made by the previous COMMIT command.
What is the function of the INDEX in SQL?
questions.answer: An INDEX in SQL is used to speed up the retrieval of data by providing a quick way to locate data.

questions.q: Which SQL command is used to remove all the rows from a table without deleting the table itself? (A) DROP TABLE (B) TRUNCATE TABLE (C) DELETE (D) UPDATE
Explain the concept of normalization in SQL and its importance.
questions.answer: Normalization is the process of organizing data in a database to minimize data redundancy and dependency. It is important because it helps to eliminate data anomalies and improve data integrity.

questions.q: State the difference between the INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN clauses in SQL.
questions.answer: The INNER JOIN clause returns only the rows that have a match in both tables, whereas the LEFT JOIN clause returns all the rows from the left table and the matching rows from the right table.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The _______ statement is used to modify existing data in a table.
